governingInstruments.fullName: Economic Crime and Corporate Transparency Act 2023, Sections 188-189
governingInstruments.scope: Indirect and direct information sharing between AML-regulated firms via third-party intermediary
governingInstruments.keyProvision: Warning condition: sharing firm must have decided (or would have decided) to take safeguarding action against the customer. Protects against confidentiality breach and civil liability.

governingInstruments.fullName: Proceeds of Crime Act 2002, Section 330 - Failure to Disclose (Regulated Sector)
governingInstruments.scope: Mandatory SAR filing obligation for regulated sector firms
governingInstruments.keyProvision: Firm must file SAR to UKFIU/NCA as soon as practicable upon knowledge or suspicion of money laundering. No financial threshold exemption for s.330 duty.

governingInstruments.fullName: Proceeds of Crime Act 2002, Section 333A - Tipping Off
governingInstruments.scope: Prohibition on disclosing SAR existence or content to subject or third parties
governingInstruments.keyProvision: Offence to disclose that SAR has been made or that investigation is underway if likely to prejudice investigation.

governingInstruments.fullName: Payment Systems Regulator Specific Direction 20 - FPS APP Scam Reimbursement Requirement
governingInstruments.scope: Mandatory APP fraud reimbursement for all PSPs on FPS and CHAPS
governingInstruments.keyProvision: All PSPs sending or receiving FPS/CHAPS payments must reimburse APP scam victims. Sending and receiving firms split costs 50:50. Maximum reimbursement £85,000.

governingInstruments.fullName: The Payment Services (Amendment) Regulations 2024 - Payment Delay Legislation
governingInstruments.scope: Allows PSPs to delay outbound payments up to 4 business days on reasonable fraud suspicion
governingInstruments.keyProvision: PSP may delay crediting transaction to payee's PSP where reasonable grounds exist to suspect fraud. Risk-based approach; must minimise impact on legitimate payments.

governingInstruments.scope: Transpose PSD2 into UK law; governs PSP obligations including fraud response
governingInstruments.keyProvision: PSPs must have robust fraud controls. Receiving PSP liability for unauthorised transactions under specific conditions.

governingInstruments.fullName: Technical Standards (Strong Customer Authentication and Common and Secure Methods of Communication) (Amendment) Instrument 2025
governingInstruments.scope: Step-up authentication requirements for payment service providers
governingInstruments.keyProvision: PSPs must revert to SCA where payment cannot be qualified as low risk. Fraud signals from intelligence platform constitute grounds for step-up trigger.

governingInstruments.fullName: UK General Data Protection Regulation, Article 25 - Data Protection by Design and by Default
governingInstruments.scope: Pseudonymisation and data minimisation obligations for all personal data processing
governingInstruments.keyProvision: Controllers must implement appropriate technical measures including pseudonymisation. Processing for fraud prevention is a legitimate interest basis under Art. 6(1)(f).

governingInstruments.fullName: National Crime Agency UK Financial Intelligence Unit - Suspicious Activity Reports Regime
governingInstruments.scope: Centralised receipt and analysis of all UK SARs
governingInstruments.keyProvision: UKFIU has sole national responsibility for receiving, analysing, and disseminating SARs. Failure to file is a criminal offence under POCA ss.330-331.

tier2.note: identifierFields present as random tokens only — not HMAC hashes

legalBasis.provision: Reasonable grounds to suspect fraud — up to 4 business day delay

delay_payment.note: Token-based account reference sufficient if receiving FI can vault-match

legalBasis.provision: PSPs must take safeguarding action; ECCTA warning condition confirms action taken

block_account.note: Token acceptable if receiving FI has vault registration

legalBasis.provision: Receiving PSP must cooperate in fraud prevention under SD20 50:50 cost-sharing
Mandatory upon knowledge or suspicion — no financial threshold for s.330 duty
legalBasis.condition: Receiving firm must form independent suspicion. Platform signal is a trigger, not a substitute for independent assessment.

legalBasis.provision: PSP must revert to SCA where payment cannot be qualified as low risk
Mandatory reimbursement within 5 business days. Max £85,000. Sending/receiving PSP split 50:50.
legalBasis.condition: paymentRail must be FPS or CHAPS

legalBasis.provision: ECCTA safe harbour covers indirect sharing via third-party intermediary under warning condition
legalBasis.condition: Warning condition must be confirmed true in signal envelope. AML-regulated firms only.

legalBasis.provision: Intelligence sharing for prevention and detection of economic crime
PSPs obliged to improve anti-fraud systems. Aggregated anonymous data not subject to GDPR restrictions.
PSR publishes fraud enabler data annually. FCA SUP 15 mandatory reporting.
PSP liability for unauthorised payment transactions under PSR 2017 Regulation 75
Mandatory SAR on suspicion — no threshold

conflictTypeDefinitions.FIELD_CLASSIFICATION: Field is a direct or indirect identifier under UK GDPR — must be suppressed or tokenised
conflictTypeDefinitions.TIPPING_OFF: Narrative or metadata contains SAR/investigation reference prohibited under POCA s.333A
conflictTypeDefinitions.K_ANONYMITY_FAILURE: Surviving categorical fields do not meet k-threshold — tier escalation required
conflictTypeDefinitions.TRANSFER_INSTRUMENT_GAP: No confirmed legal transfer mechanism for this tier between source and target jurisdictions
conflictTypeDefinitions.LEGAL_BASIS_ABSENT: Target jurisdiction lacks legal authority to act on this signal type via foreign-origin intelligence
conflictTypeDefinitions.WARNING_CONDITION_NOT_MET: ECCTA s.189 warning condition not confirmed — indirect sharing not permitted
conflictTypeDefinitions.RETENTION_CONFLICT: Source and target retention periods incompatible

tierSelectionLogic: Resolve to highest-fidelity tier that both source and target jurisdictions legally accept. UK defaults to Tier 2 for cross-border sharing where ECCTA warning condition is met and transfer mechanism is confirmed.
